source: Silver Institute World Silver Survey 2005 (GFMS Limited)
note: Pre-ETP era (SLV launched April 2006), so etf_net_flow_moz is genuinely not applicable rather than missing. Jewelry and silverware reported as one combined line ('Jewelry & Silverware'), not split. Photography demand (192.9 Moz) also included in fabrication total but not captured as a separate field here. Source table did not report an explicit net surplus/deficit headline figure; net_balance_moz left null rather than assumed.

source: Silver Institute World Silver Survey 2005 (GFMS Limited)
note: Pre-ETP era (SLV launched April 2006). Jewelry and silverware reported as one combined line, not split. Photography demand (181.0 Moz) also part of fabrication total. Net government sales and implied net investment were separate balancing items in the source table; no single headline surplus/deficit figure was given, so net_balance_moz left null rather than derived.

source: Silver Institute World Silver Survey 2016 Summary (GFMS, Thomson Reuters)
note: jewelry_silverware_moz is jewelry (224.0) + silverware (60.7) summed from the source's separate lines. total_supply_moz also includes 16.8 Moz net hedging supply not broken out in the requested fields. Later WSS2025 historical table revised mine production for nearby years, so minor cross-year inconsistencies vs. later restatements are expected.

source: Silver Institute World Silver Survey 2016 Summary (GFMS, Thomson Reuters)
note: jewelry_silverware_moz is jewelry (226.5) + silverware (62.9) summed from source. total_supply_moz includes 7.8 Moz net hedging supply not broken out separately in requested fields.

jewelry_silverware_moz is jewelry (189.1) + silverware (53.5) summed from the source's separate lines. Total supply/demand in source also include small net hedging and net official sector sales lines not separately captured here.
jewelry_silverware_moz is jewelry (196.2) + silverware (59.4) summed from source.
jewelry_silverware_moz is jewelry (203.2) + silverware (67.1) summed from source.

jewelry_silverware_moz is jewelry (201.6) + silverware (61.3) summed from source. An earlier WSS2020 press release gave slightly different preliminary figures; this row uses the later, official WSS2025 restated table instead.

jewelry_silverware_moz is jewelry (150.9) + silverware (31.2) summed from source. The original WSS2021 press release reported different totals (supply 976.2, demand 896.1, surplus +80.1); the official WSS2025 restated table shown here instead gives supply 974.0, demand 929.0, balance +45.1 — flagging this discrepancy between original press release and later restatement.

jewelry_silverware_moz is jewelry (182.0) + silverware (40.7) summed from source. The original WSS2022 press release reported different figures (mine production 822.6, supply 997.2, demand 1049.0, deficit -51.8); the official WSS2025 restated table shown here instead gives mine production 830.8, supply 1023.1, demand 1102.4, balance -79.3 — flagging this discrepancy.

jewelry_silverware_moz is jewelry (234.5) + silverware (73.5) summed from source. The original WSS2023 press release reported different figures (mine production 822.4, demand 1242, deficit -237.7); the official WSS2025 restated table shown here instead gives mine production 839.4, demand 1284.2, balance -249.6 — flagging this discrepancy.

jewelry_silverware_moz is jewelry (203.1) + silverware (55.1) summed from source. An earlier WSS2024 press release reported mine production 830.5 and a deficit in the 194-200.6 Moz range depending on source; this row uses the official WSS2025 restated table figure of mine production 812.7 and balance -200.6.
jewelry_silverware_moz is jewelry (208.7) + silverware (54.2) summed from source. This is the official published table (not a preliminary press release), so figures are treated as final for 2024.

source: Silver Institute World Silver Survey 2026 (press coverage)
note: Figures sourced from secondary press coverage of the WSS2026 press release, since the primary Silver Institute PDF returned HTTP 403 and could not be fetched directly. jewelry_silverware_moz is jewelry (189.3) + silverware (42.1) summed from that coverage. Coverage did not report a distinct ETP/ETF net flow figure (only qualitative note that 'ETF holdings also climbed significantly'), so etf_net_flow_moz is left null rather than estimated. Treat as preliminary/press-release-level precision pending cross-check against the final published WSS2026 tables.
